title: Add initial support for stream operations on queues
body:
It adds support for right stream operation on queues (and dynamic arrays) of type string. It also fixes false width warnings, when the operand is of type unpacked array.
Right stream operation is omitted in generation of c++ code, because it doesn't change the order of bits. Unfortunately, here it causes type problems, because due to the width of the operand, which is 0 in case of a queue, the cast to `IData` is inserted. I think that the result of stream operation on dynamic array-like types should be of a special type, which I added in this PR (`AstStreamDType`). I also had to add `VL_CVT_PACK_STR` function that accepts string queues as its argument.

I want to add support for stream operation on other types. I think that the best way is to cast the operands to dynamic arrays of type logic, then perform the stream operation and then cast it to the result type.
